metadata.title: Assemble a Research Packet with Source Traceability
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Source traceability converts a research packet from a reading task into a verification task.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. Source traceability converts a research packet from a reading task into a verification task.

beats.narration_text: The untraceable research packet is worse than no packet — it creates the appearance of authority without the substance. Every claim that can't be traced back to a source is a claim that has to be re-researched before acting on it. The source index is not documentation; it's what makes the packet usable.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The untraceable research packet is worse than no packet — it creates the appearance of authority without the substance. Every claim that can't be traced back to

beats.narration_text: Assemble a competitive analysis from these source documents. For every claim you include, append a source tag. At the end, produce a source index showing how many claims came from each document.

props.command: claude "Assemble a competitive analysis from these source documents. For every claim you include, append a [source: filename, section] tag. At the end, produce a source index showing how many claims came from each document." < sources/*.md

beats.narration_text: The research packet script assembles from 5 source docs, tags every claim with its source, and produces a source index with claim counts per document.

Packet assembled: 31 claims, all tagged. Source index: competitor_a.md contributed 12, competitor_b.md 9, market_report.md 7, press_releases.md 3. No claim appears without a source tag. Two claims flagged: source documents contradict each other on market share figures — CONFLICTING SOURCES flag raised.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Packet assembled: 31 claims, all tagged. Source index: competitor_a.md contributed 12, competitor_b.md 9, market_report.md 7, press_releases.md 3. No claim appe

beats.narration_text: Remove one source document and re-run; verify the packet contracts and no orphaned claims appear.

props.command: python3 research_packet.py --sources competitor_a.md market_report.md press_releases.md --output packet_v2.md

beats.narration_text: After removing competitor_b.md: packet contracts from 31 to 22 claims. Source index updates: competitor_b.md no longer appears. Zero claims from the removed source appear in the packet. The packet is smaller but fully traceable to the remaining sources. Removing a source is safe — the traceability layer enforces the contract.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: After removing competitor_b.md: packet contracts from 31 to 22 claims. Source index updates: competitor_b.md no longer appears. Zero claims from the removed sou

beats.narration_text: Every tagged claim is a claim that can be verified in one step. The source index shows which documents are doing the most work — which tells you which sources to prioritize updating. Traceability is what makes the AI's research packet a starting point rather than a trust exercise.
